title: Reviving the blog
summary: I'm back! The last time I blogged was in 2017: a lot has changed since then, and after a decade of ignoring blogging, I will attempt to put some regularity into it again. The times call for it: having a personal space that is really "our own" is extremely important, and it is as important as having something to read…

title: BetterReflection version 2.0.0 released
summary: Roave's BetterReflection 2.0.0s was released today! I and James Titcumb started working on this project back in 2015, and it is a pleasure to see it reaching maturity. The initial idea was simple: James would implement all my wicked ideas, while I would lay back and get drunk on Drambuie. Yes, that actually happened. Thank you, James, for all the…

title: Eliminating Visual Debt
summary: Today we're talking about Visual debt in our code. As an introduction, I suggest to watch this short tutorial about visual debt by @jeffrey_way. title: YubiKey for SSH, Login, 2FA, GPG and Git Signing
summary: I've been using a YubiKey Neo for a bit over two years now, but its usage was limited to 2FA and U2F. title: On Aggregates and Domain Service interaction
summary: Some time ago, I was asked where I put I/O operations when dealing with aggregates. CQRS and Event Sourced architecture, but in general, the approach that I prefer also applies to most imperative ORM entity code (assuming a proper data-mapper is involved). title: ProxyManager 2.0.0 release and expected 2.x lifetime
summary: ProxyManager 2.0.0 was finally released today! It took a bit more than a year to get here, but major improvements were included in this release, along with exclusive PHP 7 support. Most of the features that we planned to provide were indeed implemented into this release. As a negative note, HHVM compatibility was not achieved, as HHVM is not yet…

title: Doctrine ORM Hydration Performance Optimization
summary: PRE-REQUISITE: Please note that this article explains complexity in internal ORM operations with the Big-O notation. Consider reading this article, if you are not familiar with the Big-O syntax. What is hydration? Hydration when converting database results into objects. title: When to declare classes final
summary: TL;DR: Make your classes always final, if they implement an interface, and no other public methods are defined In the last month, I had a few discussions about the usage of the final marker on PHP classes. title: roave/security-advisories: Composer against Security Vulnerabilities
summary: Since it's almost christmas, it's also time to release a new project! The Roave Team is pleased to announce the release of roave/security-advisories, a package that keeps known security issues out of your project. Before telling you more, go grab it: mkdir roave-security-advisories-test cd roave-security-advisories-test curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer | php -- ./composer.phar require roave/security-advisories:dev-master What is it? roave/security-advisories is a…
